message:
fix(transport): WASM WebSocket cleanup on drop + channel stream baton-pass test

Two transport-layer improvements:

asupersync-browser-core/src/lib.rs — BrowserWebSocketHostState now
implements Drop for the wasm32 target. Previously, dropping the state
left the browser WebSocket's onmessage/onclose/onerror callbacks
pointing to freed Rust closures, which could trigger use-after-free
panics in the JS runtime when the socket received events after the Rust
side was dropped. The Drop impl clears all callbacks and closes the
socket.

src/transport/stream.rs — Removed the blanket #![allow(clippy::drop_non_drop)]
suppression (no longer needed after prior Drop impl additions).

New test: test_channel_stream_drop_wakes_next_waiter_for_pending_symbol
verifies the baton-pass protocol when a ChannelStream is dropped while
a pending symbol exists. The test sets up two receivers waiting on a
shared channel, sends one symbol (waking only the first receiver),
then drops the first receiver without consuming. This must wake the
second receiver so it can pick up the pending symbol — preventing
indefinite hangs when a consumer is dropped mid-flight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
